content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here 2‚â§K<2‚ãÖN2 \le K < 2 \cdot N2‚â§K<2‚ãÖN.**

You are given 222 integers, NNN and KKK.

Count the number of permutations PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N] satisfying the following condition:


(Pi+Pi+1)mod‚Äâ‚ÄâK‚âÝ0(P_i + P_{i + 1}) \mod K \ne 0(Pi‚Äã+Pi+1‚Äã)modKÓÄÝ=0 for all 1‚â§i<N1 \le i < N1‚â§i<N
Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid permutations modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are no valid permutations. The 222 possible permutations [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and [2,1][2, 1][2,1], both have (P1+P2)mod‚Äâ‚Äâ3=0(P_1 + P_2) \mod 3 = 0(P1‚Äã+P2‚Äã)mod3=0.
Test Case 2 : The 222 valid permutations are [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] and [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1].
